author: @DavidPaulNewtonScott
text: So You live in Canterbury home of my Universities CCU, another northerner in the south like me. Much better weather. I am thinking of one of these for my home in Grocinas Portugal. I like to go to Coimbra and go out for the day then stay in my favourite hotel for the night. We have some pretty serious hills in Coimbra but the place is gorgeous so you want to see it all. A lot of folks have scooters but you could not take them into the hotel so so one of these makes sense. I think I would go for a higher power (illegal) one say 1kw. You can also throw these in the luggage of coaches but don't ever try to fly with one the airlines will not allow it. Too big a battery.

author: @kevinconnell8422
text: Is it still holding up?

author: @estelleburgess2906
text: Did you have a throttle on the bike

author: @hneil5718
text: I always thought Paddington was more in the West of London ? But great review of the A20 ADO which as said is a very versatile bike and with a good range also. The fact that it is foldable means you can take it with you almost anywhere removing the need to carry a heavy D -Lock .

author: @anneeq008
text: So in worst case scenario how would you get this fixed? Do they have a store up north you can take it? Or do they collect it from you?

author: @cosmic.tomb.
text: You sir, are a legend.

author: @Abu_Hanzala1
text: Thanks very much Robert, I'd been looking for a video of someone who'd actually taken it on a train and you seem to be the only one I could find, so very much appreciated. Was undecided to take the plunge but will be purchasing now for riding to and from current job and also opens up options to search a little further a field for other jobs that I can reach via train and ebike.

text:
Make a ebike with bromide battery, for people like me, that live in México, lithium battery are very expensive.
Would be interesting to see the range and speed on a ebike with bromide battery  maybe would be as the lead acid battery. I havent seen any youtuber made a ebike on bromide battery.

author: @RaperJason
text:
Glad you enjoyed it Rob. Until last year I used to commute into london every day on a motorbike or an ebike depending on the weather 
You basicaly cycled round my old work stomping ground was nice to see it again but I'm glad to be away from it 😂 It looses its appeal when you do it every day sun rain and snow 😂

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yes it does - I like to visit mate - but just to visit lol

author: @hunter00047
text: I have had an electric bike for years and it's great except for one thing. You dare not park it for long as it will be gone, even with the best locks, you will always be worrying about it. We need a cheap secure locker system.

replies.author: @saifcathum3423
replies.text: I'm scared to leave my e-bike even for a few minutes. My motorcycle (Yamaha YZF-R6) on the other hand I leave right in front of the store with the keys in it for hours without a worry.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it is why I like this foldable design it can go with you a lot. more - but I get it mate - I really do

replies.author: @binmanblog
replies.text: I will suggest lockable storage units for ebikes at the Council planning meeting

author: @dav1dbone
text: Like it Rob, hope to see you out more on the bike. Whenever I get a new Ebike or add a new battery I like to go on a test run to get an idea of the range. Really cool ebike btw, over the last 14 years of ebiking there's a few decisions I've made e.g. I wouldn't have anything but disc brakes, and rear wheel drive is much safer/better than front.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate
